description: Extend CI boot-lint shellcheck to all 7 boot scripts (scripts/ + workspace/), fix the latent SC2088 in install.sh, and add a coverage probe so the gap cannot silently reopen. Harness-infra only; tracks #90.

userStories.title: Fix the SC2088 warning in scripts/install.sh
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want scripts/install.sh to be shellcheck-clean so it can be added to CI lint without introducing a failure.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a scoped '# shellcheck disable=SC2088' comment with a one-line rationale immediately above the die() line at scripts/install.sh:225, preserving the user-facing literal '~/.openharness' in the message
Do NOT substitute $HOME/.openharness — the tilde is intentional display text; substituting changes the message a user reads
No installer logic or behavior changes — only the lint-disable comment is added; the die() message string is unchanged
shellcheck -S warning scripts/install.sh exits 0 with no output
Root tests pass (pnpm test:scripts)

userStories.title: Extend the CI boot-lint shellcheck glob and its trigger paths
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the boot-lint job to shellcheck every boot script and to actually fire when those scripts change.
acceptanceCriteria: The shellcheck invocation line in .github/workflows/ci-harness.yml includes all four glob segments: .devcontainer/*.sh, install/*.sh, scripts/*.sh, workspace/*.sh
Add 'workspace/**' to BOTH on.push.paths and on.pull_request.paths in .github/workflows/ci-harness.yml (scripts/**, .devcontainer/**, install/** are already present — leave them as-is)
The hadolint step and all 'ci' job steps are unchanged
shellcheck -S warning .devcontainer/*.sh install/*.sh scripts/*.sh workspace/*.sh run from the repo root exits 0 (all seven boot scripts clean) after US-001 lands
The workflow YAML remains valid (parses cleanly)

userStories.title: Add the boot-lint-glob coverage probe
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a probe that fails if a boot-script directory is ever dropped from the lint glob, so the coverage gap cannot silently reopen.
acceptanceCriteria: evals/probes/boot-lint-glob.sh exists, is executable (chmod +x), starts with '#!/usr/bin/env bash' and 'set -euo pipefail', and carries header comments '# tier:', '# source: issue #90', '# desc:'

It locates the 'shellcheck -S warning ...' invocation line in .github/workflows/ci-harness.yml using an UNANCHORED match (the line is indented inside a 'run: |' block; a '^' anchor would match nothing) and asserts that line contains each of .devcontainer/, install/, scripts/, workspace/
Exit codes: 0=PASS (all four present), 1=REGRESSION (any missing, printed to stderr), 2=SKIPPED (workflow file absent)
No false PASS on zero-match: if the workflow file exists but no 'shellcheck -S warning' line is found, the probe exits 1 (never 0)
Renaming or removing .github/workflows/ci-harness.yml makes the probe exit 2
bash evals/probes/boot-lint-glob.sh exits 0 against the repo after US-002; temporarily deleting one glob segment makes it exit 1 (restore afterward)
Root tests pass (pnpm test:scripts)

userStories.title: Refresh evals/RESULTS.md and CHANGELOG.md
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the benchmark and changelog to reflect the new probe and fix so the work is traceable.
acceptanceCriteria: Run the eval probe suite so evals/RESULTS.md contains a row/entry for boot-lint-glob showing a PASS verdict (regenerated by the runner, not hand-faked)
No unrelated probe rows are removed from evals/RESULTS.md; the pre-existing next-dev-prod REGRESSION row is left untouched (out of scope, not introduced by this work)
CHANGELOG.md has a one-line entry under '## [Unreleased]' -> '### Fixed', imperative mood, referencing issue #90
Root tests pass (pnpm test:scripts)
